Guide.Description: The model provides a qualitative prediction for Estrogen Receptor mediated effect classification model for endocrine disruptor screening. It is implemented inside the VEGA online platform, accessible at: http://www.vega-qsar.eu/
Guide.Model: The model has been built as a set of rules, extracted with Sarpy software from a dataset obtained from a collection of high-quality estrogen receptor (ER) signaling data (1529 chemicals screened across 18 high-throughput screening assays integrated into a single score), from the ToxCast program (Judson RS et al., “Integrated model of chemical perturbations of a biological pathway using 18 in vitro high-throughput screening assays for the estrogen receptor”, Toxicol Sci 148(1):137-54, 2015). The model has been developed within the framework of the Collaborative Estrogen Receptor Activity Prediction Project (CERAPP): Mansouri K et al., “CERAPP: Collaborative Estrogen Receptor Activity Prediction Project”, Environ Health Perspect, 2016. The Sarpy software has been used with a cross-validated procedure, ending with the extraction of two sets of rules (structural alerts) related to ER-mediacted effect activity and inactivity (for a total of 59 rules). These rules have been further divided, according to their statistical significance, into a sub-set of rules with strong statistical evidence and another one of rules with weaker evidence. These rules are expressed SMARTS representing molecular fragments. If at least one rule for activity is matching with the given compound, a “Active” or “Possible active” prediction is given, depending on the statistical evidence of the rule. If no active rules are found, but at least one rule for non-activity is matching with the given compound, a “NON-Active” or “Possible NON-active” prediction is given, depending on the statistical evidence of the rule. If no rules are matching at all, no prediction is provided.

AD.Name: Similar_Molecules
AD.DescriptionRangeTop: strongly similar compounds with known experimental value in the training set have been found
AD.DescriptionRangeMid: only moderately similar compounds with known experimental value in the training set have been found
AD.DescriptionRangeBottom: no similar compounds with known experimental value in the training set have been found

AD.Name: Accuracy
AD.DescriptionRangeTop: accuracy of prediction for similar molecules found in the training set is good
AD.DescriptionRangeMid: accuracy of prediction for similar molecules found in the training set is not optimal
AD.DescriptionRangeBottom: accuracy of prediction for similar molecules found in the training set is not adequate

AD.Name: Concordance
AD.DescriptionRangeTop: similar molecules found in the training set have experimental values that agree with the predicted value
AD.DescriptionRangeMid: some similar molecules found in the training set have experimental values that disagree with the predicted value
AD.DescriptionRangeBottom: similar molecules found in the training set have experimental values that disagree with the predicted value

AD.Name: ACF_SimCheck
AD.DescriptionRangeTop: all atom centered fragment of the compound have been found in the compounds of the training set
AD.DescriptionRangeMid: some atom centered fragment of the compound have not been found in the compounds of the training set or are rare fragments
AD.DescriptionRangeBottom: a prominent number of atom centered fragments of the compound have not been found in the compounds of the training set or are rare fragments

AD.Name: AD_Index
AD.DescriptionRangeTop: predicted substance is into the Applicability Domain of the model
AD.DescriptionRangeMid: predicted substance could be out of the Applicability Domain of the model
AD.DescriptionRangeBottom: predicted substance is out of the the Applicability Domain of the model

Guide.Structural_Alerts:
The following SAs have been extracted from the original dataset and are related to ER-mediated activity: - ER activity alert no. 1: Fragment related to activity for ER-mediated effect, defined by the SMARTS: C(CC)(c1ccc(O)cc1)C(CC) - ER activity alert no. 2: Fragment related to activity for ER-mediated effect, defined by the SMARTS: Oc1ccc2C(=O)C=COc2c1 - ER activity alert no. 3: Fragment related to activity for ER-mediated effect, defined by the SMARTS: C(=Cc1ccccc1)(c2ccccc2)c3ccccc3 - ER activity alert no. 4: Fragment related to activity for ER-mediated effect, defined by the SMARTS: C12(C)CCC3C4CCC(=O)C=C4CCC3C1CCC2(O)C#C - ER activity alert no. 5: Fragment related to activity for ER-mediated effect, defined by the SMARTS: c1(ccc(C(c2ccc(O)cc2)c3ccc(O)cc3)cc1) - ER activity alert no. 6: Fragment related to activity for ER-mediated effect, defined by the SMARTS: ClC(Cl)(Cl)C(c1ccc(O)cc1)c2ccc(O)cc2 - ER activity alert no. 7: Fragment related to activity for ER-mediated effect, defined by the SMARTS: c1c(C(c2ccc(Cl)cc2)C(Cl)(Cl))c(Cl)ccc1 - ER activity alert no. 8: Fragment related to activity for ER-mediated effect, defined by the SMARTS: C(C)(c1ccc(O)cc1)(c2ccc(O)cc2)CC - ER activity alert no. 9: Fragment related to activity for ER-mediated effect, defined by the SMARTS: C(Oc1ccccc1)c2ccc(O)cc2 The following SAs have been extracted from the original dataset and are related to a possible ERmediated activity: - ER possible activity alert no. 1: Fragment related to possible activity for ER-mediated effect, defined by the SMARTS: c1(O)ccc(CCCC)cc1 - ER possible activity alert no. 2: Fragment related to possible activity for ER-mediated effect, defined by the SMARTS: C(=O)(OCC)c1ccc(O)cc1 - ER possible activity alert no. 3: Fragment related to possible activity for ER-mediated effect, defined by the SMARTS: C(c1ccc(O)cc1)c2ccc(O)cc2 - ER possible activity alert no. 4: Fragment related to possible activity for ER-mediated effect, defined by the SMARTS: C(=O)CCCCCCCCCCC(CCCC)O - ER possible activity alert no. 5: Fragment related to possible activity for ER-mediated effect, defined by the SMARTS: C(=Cc1ccccc1)(c2ccccc2) - ER possible activity alert no. 6: Fragment related to possible activity for ER-mediated effect, defined by the SMARTS: CC(C)(c1ccc(O)cc1) - ER possible activity alert no. 7: Fragment related to possible activity for ER-mediated effect, defined by the SMARTS: C(=O)c1ccc(O)cc1
The following SAs have been extracted from the original dataset and are related to ER-mediated NON-activity: - ER non-activity alert no. 1: Fragment related to non-activity for ER-mediated effect, defined by the SMARTS: C(=O)(O)CCCCC - ER non-activity alert no. 2: Fragment related to non-activity for ER-mediated effect, defined by the SMARTS: N=O - ER non-activity alert no. 3: Fragment related to non-activity for ER-mediated effect, defined by the SMARTS: n1ccccc1 - ER non-activity alert no. 4: Fragment related to non-activity for ER-mediated effect, defined by the SMARTS: CCNc1ccccc1 - ER non-activity alert no. 5: Fragment related to non-activity for ER-mediated effect, defined by the SMARTS: C(=O)NCCC - ER non-activity alert no. 6: Fragment related to non-activity for ER-mediated effect, defined by the SMARTS: C(N)O - ER non-activity alert no. 7: Fragment related to non-activity for ER-mediated effect, defined by the SMARTS: n1cncn1 - ER non-activity alert no. 8: Fragment related to non-activity for ER-mediated effect, defined by the SMARTS: c1(O)ccccc1C(C)C - ER non-activity alert no. 9: Fragment related to non-activity for ER-mediated effect, defined by the SMARTS: n1cncnc1 - ER non-activity alert no. 10: Fragment related to non-activity for ER-mediated effect, defined by the SMARTS: C(N)Nc1ccccc1 - ER non-activity alert no. 11: Fragment related to non-activity for ER-mediated effect, defined by the SMARTS: c1ncncc1 - ER non-activity alert no. 12: Fragment related to non-activity for ER-mediated effect, defined by the SMARTS: [Si](O) - ER non-activity alert no. 13: Fragment related to non-activity for ER-mediated effect, defined by the SMARTS: N=N - ER non-activity alert no. 14: Fragment related to non-activity for ER-mediated effect, defined by the SMARTS: C(CCC=C)O - ER non-activity alert no. 15: Fragment related to non-activity for ER-mediated effect, defined by the SMARTS: c1(Cl)c(Cl)cccc1 - ER non-activity alert no. 16: Fragment related to non-activity for ER-mediated effect, defined by the SMARTS: C(=C)(CCC=C(C)) - ER non-activity alert no. 17: Fragment related to non-activity for ER-mediated effect, defined by the SMARTS: c1(ccccc1)CCO - ER non-activity alert no. 18: Fragment related to non-activity for ER-mediated effect, defined by the SMARTS: c1cc(Cl)ccc1O - ER non-activity alert no. 19: Fragment related to non-activity for ER-mediated effect, defined by the SMARTS: C(=O)(N(C)C) - ER non-activity alert no. 20: Fragment related to non-activity for ER-mediated effect, defined by the SMARTS: ClCCO - ER non-activity alert no. 21: Fragment related to non-activity for ER-mediated effect, defined by the SMARTS: S(=O)(=O)N - ER non-activity alert no. 22: Fragment related to non-activity for ER-mediated effect, defined by the SMARTS: N#CCC - ER non-activity alert no. 23: Fragment related to non-activity for ER-mediated effect, defined by the SMARTS: c1(ccccc1C(=O)OCCCCCCCC)C(=O)OCCCCC - ER non-activity alert no. 24: Fragment related to non-activity for ER-mediated effect, defined by the SMARTS: c1c(C)cc(C)cc1C - ER non-activity alert no. 25: Fragment related to non-activity for ER-mediated effect, defined by the SMARTS: N(CCN)CC - ER non-activity alert no. 26: Fragment related to non-activity for ER-mediated effect, defined by the SMARTS: C(CCl)Cl - ER non-activity alert no. 27: Fragment related to non-activity for ER-mediated effect, defined by the SMARTS: c12c3ccccc3ccc1cccc2 - ER non-activity alert no. 28: Fragment related to non-activity for ER-mediated effect, defined by the SMARTS: c1ccc(Br)cc1 - ER non-activity alert no. 29: Fragment related to non-activity for ER-mediated effect, defined by the SMARTS: OCCCO - ER non-activity alert no. 30: Fragment related to non-activity for ER-mediated effect, defined by the SMARTS: n1cccc1 - ER non-activity alert no. 31: Fragment related to non-activity for ER-mediated effect, defined by the SMARTS: c1cc2c(C)cccc2cc1 - ER non-activity alert no. 32: Fragment related to non-activity for ER-mediated effect, defined by the SMARTS: CCC(F)(F)C(F)(F)C(F)(F)C(F)(F)C(F)(F)C(F)(F)F - ER non-activity alert no. 33: Fragment related to non-activity for ER-mediated effect, defined by the SMARTS: NC(C)(C)C - ER non-activity alert no. 34: Fragment related to non-activity for ER-mediated effect, defined by the SMARTS: [Si](C)C
The following SAs have been extracted from the original dataset and are related to a possible ERmediated NON-activity: - ER possible non-activity alert no. 1: Fragment related to possible non-activity for ER-mediated effect, defined by the SMARTS: CCOCC - ER possible non-activity alert no. 2: Fragment related to possible non-activity for ER-mediated effect, defined by the SMARTS: C(=O)(O)CCC - ER possible non-activity alert no. 3: Fragment related to possible non-activity for ER-mediated effect, defined by the SMARTS: C(O)CO - ER possible non-activity alert no. 4: Fragment related to possible non-activity for ER-mediated effect, defined by the SMARTS: Nc1ccccc1 - ER possible non-activity alert no. 5: Fragment related to possible non-activity for ER-mediated effect, defined by the SMARTS: SC - ER possible non-activity alert no. 6: Fragment related to possible non-activity for ER-mediated effect, defined by the SMARTS: NCc1ccccc1 - ER possible non-activity alert no. 7: Fragment related to possible non-activity for ER-mediated effect, defined by the SMARTS: Nc1ccc(C)cc1 - ER possible non-activity alert no. 8: Fragment related to possible non-activity for ER-mediated effect, defined by the SMARTS: c1ccccc1Cl - ER possible non-activity alert no. 9: Fragment related to possible non-activity for ER-mediated effect, defined by the SMARTS: C(=O)
